paper_focus.title: Dynamic recrystallization mechanisms of an Fe–8% Al low density steel under hot rolling conditions
paper_focus.research_objective: To elucidate the dynamic recrystallization mechanisms in a Fe-8% Al low-density steel during hot deformation and their dependence on strain, strain rate, and temperature, in order to control microstructure and minimize roping.
material_systems: Fe-8% Al ferritic steel (nominal composition: Fe-8.15% Al, 0.1% Mn, 0.0086% C, <0.1% Ti+Nb+V)
main_process_variables: Post-deformation quenching with argon (0.5 s delay, ~200 °C/s cooling rate)
Observation at 0.9 radius (near surface) on θz plane
expected_information_axis: microstructure (EBSD maps, grain boundaries, subgrains, crystallite aggregates, texture pole figures)
recrystallization mechanism (CDRX vs DDRX, transition conditions)
